sections.paragraph:
f. with suff. תֹּוכַחְתִּי, pl. תּוכָחֹות (from the root יָכַח).

(1) the act of arguing, shewing and maintaining the right, Jud. 16:6 . Pl. arguments, Job 23:4.

(2) arguing down, contradicting, Psa 38:15 Pro 29:1, אִישׁ תֹּוכָחֹות “a man who opposes in speaking,” one who likes to speak against, positive in assertions (others take it from signif. 3, who is often corrected).

(3) rebuke, correction by words, Pro 1:23, 25 , 30 3:11 5:12 27:5 29:15 . Plur. תֹּוכְחֹות מוּסָר rebuke joined with correction. Pro 6:23. Once used of reproof (from God), complaint, Hab 2:1.

(4) punishment, chastening, Psa 73:14. Pl. Psa 39:12 Eze 5:15 תֹּוכְחֹות חֵמָה Eze 25:17.
